text: Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Protsahan (PM-USP) Yojana: This central sector scheme provides financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker backgrounds to pursue higher education. Eligibility: Students must have scored at least 80 percentile in Class 12 and have a family income less than ₹4.5 lakh per annum. Benefits: Financial support for undergraduate and postgraduate courses, including tuition fees and maintenance allowances.

PM-Vidyalaxmi Education Loan Scheme: Offers collateral-free and guarantor-free education loans to meritorious students for higher education in top institutions. Eligibility: Meritorious students admitted to eligible higher education institutions. Family income up to ₹8 lakhs for interest subvention. Benefits: Loans up to ₹10 lakhs with interest subvention (4% for income < ₹8L). No collateral required for loans up to ₹7.5 lakhs.

National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship Scheme (NMMSS): Aimed at reducing dropout rates after Class 8 by providing financial support to meritorious students. Eligibility: Class 9 students with family income < ₹3.5 lakh. Must score at least 55% in Class 8. Benefits: Scholarship of ₹12,000 per annum (₹1,000 per month) from Class 9 to Class 12.

Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY): A girl child savings scheme to encourage parents to build a fund for her future education and marriage. Eligibility: Account can be opened by parents/guardians for a girl child below 10 years. Only two accounts per family (three in case of twins/triplets). Benefits: High interest rate (8.2% for Q4 2023-24), tax benefits under Section 80C, and partial withdrawal allowed for higher education after age 18.
